generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LONICERA. Lonicera Xylosteum, Linn. Natural order , Caprifoliaceæ. Common names , Fly-woodbine; (G.), Heckenkirsche, Teufels-kirsche, etc.; (Fr.), La chèvre-feuille des haies. Preparation , Triturations of the berries. Authorities. 1 , Jahn. Kasp. Woch., 1834 (Frank's Mag., 1, 247), poisoning of three children by the berries; 2 , Blattmann, Schw. Zeit., 1841 (Fr. Mag., 3, 551) a child poisoned by the berries; 3 , same, effects in an apothecary from evaporating an infusion; 4 , Duval, Bib. Hom., 5, p. 386, two cases of poisoning by the berries; 5 , Buzorini, Med. Corr. Bl. Wurt., 1850. poisoning of a boy by the berries. MIND. ► Stupefaction, [1] . ► Coma, [4] . HEAD. ► Great congestion of the head and chest, [2] . EYE. ► Conjunctiva red, [2] . ► Pupils dilated, [2] . ► Contraction of one pupil and dilatation of the other, [4] . ► Such weakness of vision that for half an hour everything seemed foggy and indistinct, [3] . ► Photophobia, [2] . FACE. ► Red face, [2] . [10.] ► Pale face, [2] . ► Lips dry, [2] . MOUTH. ► Tongue moist, coated with mucus, [2] . STOMACH. ► Excessive thirst, [1] . ► Vomiting, [4] , [5] . ► Vomiting and purging, [2] . ► Profuse vomiting and purging, [1] . ABDOMEN. ► Abdomen retracted, especially in the umbilical region; soft, [2] . ► Violent colic, [1] . STOOL. ► Bloody stools, [1] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. [20.] ► Respiration rapid and deep, [2] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Violent impulse of the heart, [2] . ► Slowness of the pulse, [4] . ► Pulse small soft, irregular, [2] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Frequent jerking of the limbs, [2] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Seemed to neither hear nor see; was unable to make an answer, [5] . ► Trembling and jerking of the whole body, [1] . ► Violent convulsions, [1] . ► When raised from the bed the limbs and head fell over as it paralyzed, [5] . SLEEP. ► Sleepiness, [2] . [30.] ► Constant deep sleep with half-open eyes, [2] . ► Lay with half-open eyes, red face, in a soporous condition, [5] . FEVER. ► Extremities cold, [2] . ► Fever, [1] . ► Profuse cold perspiration, [1] . Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
